title: Direct and Spillover Effects of Middle School Vaccination Requirements
notes: Stata CSDID: csdid2 P_U13TDAP $individ_controls i.age i.fips [w=provwt], window(-6 5). fips = STATE dummies (51 states, NOT counties). cs_controls reduced to female+married (many controls cause singularity in att_gt). CS-NT ≈ CS-NYT in R (both ~0.095): R att_gt(panel=FALSE) distinguishes NT from NYT at ATT(g,t) cell level (62/72 cells differ), but positive and negative differences cancel in aggte(simple) aggregation — dataset-specific cancellation, not a structural bug. R NYT (~0.093) ≈ Stata NYT (0.099, ~6% diff, credible). R NT (~0.095) ≠ Stata NT (0.164) — likely due to different NT pool composition: early cohorts (MN gvar=1996, RI gvar=1999) may be in Stata's NT pool but are dropped in R as 'already treated at first period'. Stata NT has significant pre-trends (Pre_avg=-0.048, p=0.001) suggesting parallel trends failure for NT comparison; NYT is the more credible estimator (Pre_avg=-0.022, p=0.143, insignificant). See failure_patterns.md Pattern 24. BJS: Stata uses did_imputation with fe(fips year12 age); R aggregates to (fips×year12) panel via weighted.mean first — approximation (age FEs lost). Stata BJS horizons(0/6) pretrends(5). Event window: -6 to +6.
